$SPCX has now landed Starlink Wi-Fi deals with three of the four largest U.S. airlines in United, Southwest and American.

That puts Starlink on track for 2,300+ commercial aircraft.

$NVDA CEO Jensen Huang and $TSM CEO C.C. Wei had dinner ahead of COMPUTEX.

Would kill to be a fly on the wall at that dinner with one side designing the AI boom and the other side manufacturing it.

OpenAI is reportedly building ChatGPT ads for small businesses by expanding into local advertisers like car washes and dry cleaners.

The rollout includes no minimum spend for some buyers with OpenAI reportedly targeting $2.4B in ad revenue this year and $102B by 2030.

$NVDA is now worth more than $AVGO, $AMD, $MU, $QCOM, $TXN, $INTC, $AMAT, $LRCX and $KLAC combined.

Thats what happens when one company sits at the platform layer of AI and captures dollar content across GPUs, networking, software, automotive, robotics and Physical AI.
$MU OFFICIALLY JOINS THE $1T MARKET CAP CLUB
These 2027 $MU leaps I bought just two months ago are now up nearly 10x  🤯
NASDAQ 100 JUST CROSSED 30,000 FOR THE FIRST TIME EVER
A $10K investment in $MU at the IPO would be worth ~$9.2M today  🤯

$AAL is partnering with $SPCX to install Starlink Wi-Fi across 500+ Airbus aircraft.

The deal brings satellite internet deeper into commercial aviation and expands Starlink’s role as the connectivity layer for planes, ships and remote infrastructure.

$QCOM reportedly struck a deal to supply ByteDance with AI-focused ASICs potentially making TikTok’s parent one of Qualcomm’s first major AI infrastructure customers.

The deal would move Qualcomm beyond smartphones and into AI agent compute.

I added $NVTS last year to my 'AI Power' bucket because Navitas was shifting from low-end mobile and consumer markets into the high-power systems that matter for AI infrastructure.

What made it interesting was that Navitas owns both GaN and SiC giving it exposure to two critical power technologies as SiC brings massive power into AI systems and GaN converts that power efficiently closer to the chip.

The stock is now up nearly 15x since my entry.

$AVGO launched the BCM68850 which is a new chip that turns your home internet router into a mini AI computer.

Broadcom says it brings symmetric 50G speeds, post-quantum security and AI inference directly to the broadband edge.

"I'm not saying to buy this thing, I'm just saying this is how you get things going."

The repost details the Wall Street consensus that the Strait is days away from reopening, and this will lead to crude prices plummeting.

Add this chart. It shows that most of the net long positions among managed money (CTAs) and Large Speculators were established BEFORE the war began (and made good money), and they have been paring it back since the opening days of the war (prices are higher today). 

Restated, Wall Street's peak oil bullishness was over two months ago, and they have been pulling back every week. Industry professionals (i.e., Commercial Traders) are on the other side of Wall Street.

Which side do you want to be on?

📺 TIME TO SELL INTO STRENGTH? + $AMZN, $GOOGL & $ASTS CATCH-UP TRADES

The market is becoming increasingly extended after a powerful rally off the April lows. 

It reclaimed the 8/21-day MAs after April 7th and has largely stayed above them ever since, with the $SPY now pressing into prior highs around $749.53–$751.45.

The key question now is whether the market can continue building above resistance after the ceasefire-driven rally or whether traders will begin “selling the news.”

I would be selling into strength after a large gap-up, while observing whether the market can hold above prior highs and consolidate constructively.

*

I am currently holding 11 long positions along with multiple options positions while maintaining only modest hedges, including $SPY short premium exposure around the $749 area and a small $TSLA hedge via $TSLL.

The plan is to continue participating in the uptrend while remaining flexible, as the market is now extended above short-term moving averages.

The probability of consolidation or a pullback, even if the primary trend remains bullish, is very high right now.

*

There are a few setups on my radar right now – lagging mega-cap names that could potentially play catch-up if the rally broadens further.

$AMZN is the first one. While many leading names had already broken to new highs, Amazon had stalled after a strong move toward the $278 area earlier in May and had since been consolidating.

Here is a tactical options strategy: buying slightly out-of-the-money calls several weeks out — like July $280 calls — under the thesis that if #AMZN is truly going to reassert leadership, it should reclaim and hold above $278.50.

The same framework can be applied to $GOOGL. Google had initially been one of the strongest names during the earlier phase of the rally, leading aggressively after reclaiming the $311 level and pushing toward the $403–$408 region.

However, unlike many AI-related momentum stocks, #GOOGL had spent recent weeks consolidating sideways and pulling back toward the $381 area.

If Google can eventually rotate toward $425, you should consider initiating a smaller call position before the stock fully breaks out rather than waiting for confirmation at higher prices.

*

Here is another important point – the value of re-engaging quality momentum names during periods of weakness rather than abandoning them after setbacks.

$ASTS is a great example.  After a failed launch and sharp pullback, #ASTS had become unpopular, but I began buying into weakness around the low $100-s and $105 area based on the belief that it could eventually recover back toward $129 or higher.

This gradual re-engagement allowed me to rebuild exposure before the stock reclaimed key moving averages and resumed momentum. 

However, despite remaining bullish, I want to emphasize disciplined profit-taking. Because $ASTS had already produced substantial gains and moved into an extended condition, the plan is to trim exposure into strength.

REMEMBER: In environments with strong momentum, traders should actively manage winners rather than simply hoping for continued upside.

*

Hedges also play a critical role.

I was rolling short $SPY hedge positions higher — specifically moving from short $749 calls toward $754 calls — in order to maintain upside participation while still carrying some protection in case the market suddenly reverses.

This strategy allows me to create a buffer in which long positions could continue to benefit if the market drifts higher, while the hedge would only begin offsetting gains beyond higher resistance levels.

So, stay involved in the bullish trend but combine it with tactical trimming, disciplined hedging, and selective re-entry into consolidating leaders rather than chasing extended names.

If you found this helpful, please ❤️like and 🔁retweet

$SPX is opening about +50 at a new all-time high. 7517 is the key level to watch-if it holds and builds, the uptrend stays intact. If it fails, a retrace could bring 7418 into play first, with 7330 as the next more important support area.

Marking 8 Years of the GraniteShares COMB ETF

Today marks 8 years since the launch of the GraniteShares Bloomberg Commodity Broad Strategy No K-1 ETF (COMB).

Over the years, COMB has provided investors with access to a broad basket of commodities through an exchange-traded structure designed to simplify commodity investing without issuing a Schedule K-1 tax form.
